title: Smoky Tangy German Sauerkraut Gratin
description: A citrusy, silky, kid-friendly German dish featuring smoky paprika, temper, and is keto-friendly. This recipe is great for weeknights when you need a comforting meal quickly.
instructions: Shred the cabbage and carrots in a food processor.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the cabbage mixture, smoked paprika, garlic, caraway seeds, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the cabbage mixture to a baking dish and cover with heavy cream and shredded cheddar cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the sauce to thicken.
This smoky, tangy German dish is a kid-friendly favorite and is keto-friendly due to the use of heavy cream and cheddar cheese instead of potatoes.
The caraway seeds add a unique flavor and aroma to the dish, making it a great alternative to traditional German cuisine.
The smoked paprika adds a smoky flavor and a pop of color to the dish, making it visually appealing.
This recipe is great for weeknights when you need a comforting meal quickly and is easy to make with just a few ingredients.
This dish is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a special occasion and can be served with a side of rye bread or a salad for a complete meal.
Serve the Smoky Tangy German Sauerkraut Gratin warm and enjoy!
This recipe makes 4-6 servings and can be easily doubled or tripled to feed a larger crowd.
The dish is easy to make and requires just 30 minutes of prep time and 25-30 minutes of cook time, making it a great option for busy weeknights.
The recipe is also keto-friendly and can be adapted to suit different dietary needs by adjusting the amount of heavy cream used.
The smoky flavor of the paprika pairs perfectly with the tangy flavor of the cabbage and the richness of the cream, making it a unique and delicious dish.
